ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific class for fruit-and-nut enriched bread; 'bread food product' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 category A000E (Bread and rolls) is the closest defensible match; no specific sub-code exists for fruit-and-nut bread in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for fruit-and-nut bread; omitted to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of fruit and nut bread, approximately 35 g, providing around 110 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Bread with fruits and nuts — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05030003 is sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 and review_status 'reviewed'. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, mixed-grain' (NDB 18023) as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HPB-specific 'Bread with fruits and nuts'. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed-grain or wholemeal bread with dried fruit inclusions (GI range 60–70 reported for enriched wheat-based breads). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 65 × 18.4 / 100 ≈ 12.0 (serving size 35 g, available carbohydrate = 52.6 × 0.35 = 18.4 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 285 mg/100 g is in the borderline range for hypertension management; a 35 g slice contributes approximately 100 mg sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one slice (35 g) per meal and choose low-sodium bread formulations where available, as sodium at 285 mg/100 g approaches the threshold that warrants caution in a sodium-restricted diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Total carbohydrate of 52.6 g/100 g with a medium GI of approximately 65 produces a glycemic load of ~12 per 35 g slice, which is significant for blood glucose management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict intake to one thin slice (30–35 g) per meal, pair with a protein source such as eggs or nut butter to blunt the glycemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose, as the moderate-to-high carbohydrate density and medium GI of this bread can elevate postprandial glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low (~1.2 g/100 g estimated), dietary fibre is 3.8 g/100 g, and the inclusion of nuts provides heart-healthy unsaturated fatty acids; sodium at 285 mg/100 g warrants portion awareness.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This bread is broadly suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed in controlled portions (one slice, 35 g), as the low saturated fat content, moderate fibre, and nut-derived unsaturated fats support a heart-healthy dietary pattern, though sodium intake should be monitored across the full day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nut content provides ALA omega-3 fatty acids and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties; dietary fibre at 3.8 g/100 g supports gut microbiome health relevant to immune regulation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from this bread when it contains walnuts or almonds, as these provide anti-inflammatory ALA omega-3 fatty acids; choose versions with unsweetened dried fruits and low sodium to minimise pro-inflammatory dietary load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bread with fruits and nuts is a baked wheat-based loaf enriched with dried fruits (such as raisins, cranberries, or apricots) and whole or chopped nuts (such as walnuts, almonds, or cashews). It is produced by incorporating these inclusions into a standard yeast-leavened bread dough before baking, resulting in a dense, moist crumb with a slightly sweet flavour from the dried fruit and a pleasant crunch from the nuts. Per 100 g, this bread provides 314 kcal, 12.6 g protein, 7.4 g fat, 52.6 g carbohydrate, 3.8 g dietary fibre, 285 mg sodium, 119 mg calcium, and 2.18 mg iron. The protein content is notably high for a bread product, reflecting the contribution of nuts. Dietary fibre at 3.8 g/100 g supports digestive health and moderates glycemic response. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium at 285 mg/100 g is borderline for hypertension management. The inclusion of nuts contributes heart-healthy unsaturated fats and anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids (ALA). This bread is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or snack in Singapore and internationally, and is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. It contains gluten and tree nuts as major allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030003, review_status 'reviewed', confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as an approved secondary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: 1. sugar_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB source. This is a confirmed value from the approved source; however, it is noted that dried fruit inclusions typically contribute measurable sugars, and this value may reflect a specific product formulation or a data gap in the HPB record. Users should verify sugar content against the specific product label. 2. saturated_fat_g (1.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Bread, mixed-grain' (NDB 18023) as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this specific food item (F05030003) and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to provide defensible estimates for all fields. 4. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as standard baked bread without hydrogenated fats contains negligible trans fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for comparable bread products. 5. cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 as this is a plant-based bread product containing no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific HPB product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 7. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory multilingual naming; it is an internationally generic bread product, so local_names fields are null and aliases are English-only, which is compliant with the multilingual rules.
